title: verbascum erianthum
intro: Verbascum erianthum, also known as Woolly Mullein or Bear's Grease, is a biennial plant species belonging to the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its tall, upright stems and large, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Verbascum erianthum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Verbascum erianthum can be found in teas, infusions, salves, and ointments, often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
